claims.flip_evidence_text: excluding, solely in the case of clause (x), any effect resulting directly or indirectly from (i) changes in GAAP or the official interpretation thereof
claims.notes: The exclusion applies only to clause (x) (financial condition, etc.) and not to clause (y) (ability to perform obligations). It is also subject to a disproportionate-adverse-effect exception for clauses (i), (ii), (iii), and (v).

prompt:
You are a proxy legal annotator for a research pilot. Use only the contract excerpt, the question, the model answer, and the fixed claim list.

For each claim return:
- correctness_label: true, false, or partially_true
- load_bearing_label: yes or no
- flip_evidence_text: required if load_bearing_label=yes, else empty string
- notes: optional short note

Contract excerpt:
“Company Material Adverse Effect” means a material adverse effect on (x) the financial condition, assets, business or results of operations of the Company and its Subsidiaries, taken as a whole or (y) the ability of the Company and its Subsidiaries to perform their obligations under, or to consummate the transactions contemplated by, this Agreement, excluding, solely in the case of clause (x), any effect resulting directly or indirectly from (i) changes in GAAP or the official interpretation thereof, (ii) general economic, political, regulatory, legal or tax conditions in the United States or any other country or region, including changes in financial, credit, securities or currency markets (including changes in interest or exchange rates), (iii) conditions generally affecting the industries in which the Company and its Subsidiaries operate, (iv) changes in Applicable Law or the interpretation thereof, (v) geopolitical conditions, the outbreak or escalation of hostilities, acts of war, sabotage, terrorism, cyberattacks, natural disasters, acts of god, demonstrations, public disaster, epidemics, pandemics or other diseases (including COVID-19 and any COVID-19 Measures) including any deterioration or worsening thereof, (vi) the announcement, pendency, or consummation of the transactions contemplated by this Agreement or the announcement of Parent’s plans or intentions with respect to the conduct of the business of the Company following Closing, including the impact of any of the foregoing on the relationships, contractual or otherwise, of the Company and any of its Subsidiaries with customers, suppliers, service providers, employees, Governmental Authorities or any other Persons and any stockholder or derivative litigation relating to the execution, delivery and performance of this Agreement or the announcement or consummation of the transactions contemplated by this Agreement, (vii) any failure by the Company or any of its Subsidiaries to meet any internal or published budgets, projections, forecasts or predictions of financial performance or integration synergies for any period (it being understood that any underlying facts giving rise or contributing to such failure that are not otherwise excluded from the definition of a “Company Material Adverse Effect” may be taken into account in determining whether there has been a Company Material Adverse Effect), (viii) any actions taken (or omitted to be taken) by the Company or any of its Subsidiaries in order to comply with the obligations contained in Section 8.01 or at the written request of Parent or Merger Sub, or (ix) changes in the price and/or trading volume of the shares of Company Common Stock or any other securities of the Company on NYSE or any other market on which such securities are quoted for purchase and sale or changes in the credit ratings of the Company (it being understood that any underlying facts giving rise or contributing to such changes that are not otherwise excluded from the definition of a “Company Material Adverse Effect” may be taken into account in determining whether there has been a Company Material Adverse Effect) or (x) any actions taken (or omitted to be taken) by the Company or any of its Subsidiaries that are required to be taken (or omitted to be taken) pursuant to this Agreement, including any actions required under this Agreement to obtain any approvals, consents, registrations, permits, authorizations and other confirmations under applicable Competition Laws and Foreign Investment Laws for the consummation of the Merger, except, with respect to clauses (i), (ii), (iii) and (v), to the extent that such event has had a disproportionate adverse effect on the Company or any of its Subsidiaries relative to other companies operating in the industry or industries in which the Company or any of its Subsidiaries conducts business, in which case the incremental disproportionate adverse impact may be taken into account in determining whether there has occurred or would reasonably be expected to occur a Company Material Adverse Effect. (Page 4)

Question:
Changes in GAAP or other accounting principles (Y/N)

Llama answer:
Yes, changes in GAAP or the official interpretation thereof are excluded from the definition of a "Company Material Adverse Effect".

Canonical claims:
- claim-6d31248162fd: Yes, changes in GAAP or the official interpretation thereof are excluded from the definition of a "Company Material Adverse Effect".

You are a proxy legal annotator for a research pilot. Use only the contract excerpt, the question, the model answer, and the fixed claim list.

For each claim return:
- correctness_label: true, false, or partially_true
- load_bearing_label: yes or no
- flip_evidence_text: required if load_bearing_label=yes, else empty string
- notes: optional short note

“Company Material Adverse Effect” means a material adverse effect on (x) the financial condition, assets, business or results of operations of the Company and its Subsidiaries, taken as a whole or (y) Question:
Changes in GAAP or other accounting principles (Y/N)

Llama answer:
Yes, changes in GAAP or the official interpretation thereof are excluded from the definition of a "Company Material Adverse Effect".

message.reasoning:
The user wants me to act as a proxy legal annotator. I need to evaluate the canonical claim against the contract excerpt, question, and model answer.

 Contract excerpt defines "Company Material Adverse Effect" and excludes, solely in the case of clause (x), any effect resulting directly or indirectly from (i) changes in GAAP or the official interpretation thereof... except with respect to clauses (i), (ii), (iii) and (v), to the extent of disproportionate adverse effect.

 The question is: "Changes in GAAP or other accounting principles (Y/N)"

 The Llama answer is: "Yes, changes in GAAP or the official interpretation thereof are excluded from the definition of a 'Company Material Adverse Effect'."

 The canonical claim is: "Yes, changes in GAAP or the official interpretation thereof are excluded from the definition of a 'Company Material Adverse Effect'."

 I need to determine:
 1. correctness_label: true, false, or partially_true
 2. load_bearing_label: yes or no
 3. flip_evidence_text: required if load_bearing_label=yes, else empty string
 4. notes: optional short note

 Analysis:
 - The claim states that changes in GAAP or the official interpretation thereof are excluded from the definition of a "Company Material Adverse Effect".
 - Looking at the contract: "excluding, solely in the case of clause (x), any effect resulting directly or indirectly from (i) changes in GAAP or the official interpretation thereof... except, with respect to clauses (i), (ii), (iii) and (v), to the extent that such event has had a disproportionate adverse effect on the Company..."
 - So the exclusion is not absolute. It is subject to the disproportionate impact exception. The claim says they are "excluded" without mentioning the exception.
 - However, the claim is essentially restating the Llama answer. Is it true? The exclusion does exist, but it's qualified. The claim is technically partially true because it omits the important qualification that the exclusion doesn't apply to the extent there's a disproportionate adverse effect relative to others in the industry.
 - Wait, let me think about how these annotations usually work. The claim is a direct quote of the Llama answer. The Llama answer says "Yes, changes in GAAP... are excluded". This is generally true as a statement but incomplete. In legal annotation tasks, if a claim omits a material qualification, it's often labeled "partially_true" or "true" depending on whether the omission makes it misleading.
 - The exclusion is real: changes in GAAP are indeed excluded from clause (x), subject to the disproportionate impact carve-out. The claim captures the main point but misses the exception.
 - Given the context of Y/N question, the answer "Yes" is correct (they are excluded), but the full picture includes the exception.
 - I think "partially_true" is appropriate because the exclusion is subject to a disproportionate effect exception, which is material.

 Now, load_bearing_label: Does the claim rely on specific evidence text that, if flipped/changed, would change the label?
 - The claim is supported by the text: "excluding, solely in the case of clause (x), any effect resulting directly or indirectly from (i) changes in GAAP or the official interpretation thereof"
 - If this text were absent or said the opposite, the claim would be false. So yes, it is load-bearing.
 - The flip_evidence_text should be the text that supports the claim. Usually, it's the relevant excerpt. Let me provide the key part.

 Actually, looking at the instruction: "flip_evidence_text: required if load_bearing_label=yes, else empty string". This likely means the text from the contract that bears the load of supporting the claim (i.e., the evidence). If that evidence were "flipped" (negated), the claim would change. So I should provide the exact or approximate text from the contract that supports it.

 The supporting text is: "excluding, solely in the case of clause (x), any effect resulting directly or indirectly from (i) changes in GAAP or the official interpretation thereof"

 Wait, but there's also the exception: "except, with respect to clauses (i), (ii), (iii) and (v), to the extent that such event has had a disproportionate adverse effect..." This is why it's partially true. The flip evidence should probably be the main exclusion text.

 Let me reconsider correctness. The question is "Changes in GAAP or other accounting principles (Y/N)". This seems to be asking whether changes in GAAP are excluded. The answer "Yes" is correct in the general sense. However, the precise legal answer is "Yes, but subject to disproportionate impact exception." Since the claim omits the exception, is it false? In many legal annotation schemes, an incomplete but directionally correct statement is "partially_true". I think that's the safest label here.

 Let me check if there's any other nuance. The exclusion is "solely in the case of clause (x)". Clause (x) refers to "financial condition, assets, business or results of operations". It does NOT exclude from clause (y), which is "ability to perform obligations/consummate transactions". So changes in GAAP that affect ability to perform obligations under the agreement are NOT excluded. The claim says they are excluded from the definition generally ("are excluded from the definition of a 'Company Material Adverse Effect'"). This is another reason why it's not fully true. They are only excluded from the clause (x) prong, not the clause (y) prong.

 So the claim is partially true because:
 1. The exclusion applies only to clause (x), not clause (y).
 2. The exclusion is subject to a disproportionate impact exception.

 Therefore, correctness_label: partially_true.

 Load-bearing: yes. The evidence is the exclusion clause (i).

 Flip evidence text: "excluding, solely in the case of clause (x), any effect resulting directly or indirectly from (i) changes in GAAP or the official interpretation thereof"

 Notes: Should mention that the exclusion is limited to clause (x) and subject to a disproportionate impact exception.

 Let me format the output. It seems the user wants the result for each claim. There's only one claim: claim-6d31248162fd.

 Output format should probably be something like:
 claim-6d31248162fd:
   correctness_label: partially_true
   load_bearing_label: yes
   flip_evidence_text: "excluding, solely in the case of clause (x), any effect resulting directly or indirectly from (i) changes in GAAP or the official interpretation thereof"
   notes: Exclusion applies only to clause (x) and is subject to disproportionate impact exception.

 Or maybe JSON/YAML? The user didn't specify a strict format, but gave the fields. I'll provide a clear structured output.

 Wait, the user said "For each claim return:". I'll return the fields clearly labeled for the claim ID.

 Let me double-check the exact wording for flip_evidence_text. It should probably be the exact contract text. I'll use the relevant snippet.

 Actually, looking at the contract, the full relevant part is: "excluding, solely in the case of clause (x), any effect resulting directly or indirectly from (i) changes in GAAP or the official interpretation thereof"

 And later: "except, with respect to clauses (i), (ii), (iii) and (v), to the extent that such event has had a disproportionate adverse effect on the Company or any of its Subsidiaries relative to other companies operating in the industry or industries in which the Company or any of its Subsidiaries conducts business..."

 So my notes can be concise.

 Final check:
 - correctness_label: partially_true (because exclusion is only for clause (x) and subject to disproportionate impact carve-out)
 - load_bearing_label: yes
 - flip_evidence_text: excluding, solely in the case of clause (x), any effect resulting directly or indirectly from (i) changes in GAAP or the official interpretation thereof
 - notes: The exclusion is limited to clause (x) and subject to a disproportionate-adverse-effect exception.

 This looks solid.
